title: Protocol Guidance for Automated Isolation of Viral DNA/RNA in Deep Well Plates SKU T4010
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5iofe","text":"This protocol details the automated isolation of viral DNA and RNA in deep well plates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0rvs","text":"General guidance for automated viral DNA/RNA purification in 1.0 ml, 96-well deep well plates: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":95}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgft7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bg6gc","text":"A detailed KingFisher Flex automation protocol with appropriate buffer volumes is provided in the Appendices.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dbgt4","text":"Refer to the product webpage for the most up-to-date information on additional guidance and protocols for other automation/liquid handling instruments.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"81e3b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"819u1","text":"Important Notes Before You Begin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3rtp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39hik","text":"Review Reagent Preparation section.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cngf0","text":"Store Proteinase K at   upon receipt.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"87bf","text":"Prepare Monarch Carrier RNA based on kit size used: Add   (NEB #T4010S) or  (NEB #T4010L/X) nuclease-free water, invert or pipette to mix, and transfer to an RNase-free microfuge tube. Keep on ice. Prepare single-use aliquots and store at  . elution).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"esuqq","text":"Plastics (e.g., 96-well deep well plates) must be compatible with the workflow.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2tbp3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ltru","text":"Reagent RNA:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2v3ur","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"emk12","text":"For reconstitution of Carrier RNA based on kit size used, add 125 µl (NEB #T4010S) or 750 µl (NEB #T4010L/X) nuclease-free water, invert or pipette to mix, and transfer to an RNase-free microfuge tube. Keep on ice. Prepare single-use aliquots and store at –20°C. Avoid multiple freeze-thaw cycles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bvjqb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8e8b4","text":"Lysis Buffer Bead Mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvelv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1enj","text":"Prepare Lysis Buffer Bead Mix immediately before use (i.e., immediately before Sample Lysis, or during the Sample Lysis, Proteinase K incubation step).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7h83c","text":"Prepare Lysis Buffer Bead Mix by combining Monarch StabiLyse DNA/RNA Buffer (included), Monarch Carrier RNA (included), Isopropanol (user supplied), and Monarch Mag Beads M1 (included) as described in the protocol, adding components in the order listed. Vortex magnetic beads ~20 seconds immediately before use to form a homogeneous suspension. Carefully open the bottle after vortexing to ensure the magnetic beads do not spill.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h5cp","text":"Store Lysis Buffer Bead Mix at room temperature.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bndfr","text":"If preparing a master mix, prepare excess to ensure there is sufficient volume of the mixture for each reaction. We recommend an overage of up to 15%.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8brq1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"86n6v","text":"User-Prepared Wash Buffers:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49qnh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7i0c6","text":"Viral DNA/RNA Wash Buffer: Prepare Viral DNA/RNA Wash Buffer in a user-supplied tube or bottle (free of nucleases) by combining Monarch Buffer BX (included), nuclease-free water (included), and Isopropanol (user supplied) as described in the protocol, adding components in the order listed. The nuclease-free water included in the kit is intended to be used for preparing this wash buffer. Users are instructed to prepare an amount of wash buffer that includes up to 15% excess to ensure sufficient volume for each reaction. Prepare Viral DNA/RNA Wash Buffer fresh, as a precipitate may form upon storage. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jq3t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfphr","text":"80% Ethanol: Prepare 80% fresh ethanol using 100% ethanol (user supplied) and nuclease-free water (user supplied) in a user-supplied bottle, free of nucleases. Users are instructed to prepare an amount of 80% ethanol that includes up to 15% excess to ensure sufficient volume for each reaction. To support our sustainability efforts, we have not included additional bottles and nuclease-free water for these 80% ethanol wash steps to avoid shipping excessive materials that may not apply to all users.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-20","unit":10}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"125","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}},"2":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"750","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}},"3":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-20","unit":10}},"4":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"320","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}}}}

steps: “Please review the important information under the “Guidelines & Warnings”. Ensure the automation instrument is equipped with the appropriate hardware (e.g., magnet, shaker, heat block).

Load an appropriate script onto the instrument that aligns with sample, wash, elution volumes, and sample processing steps (e.g., sample/bead mixing, bead collection, supernatant removal, wash steps, bead drying, and heated elution).
Ensure instrument-compatible plastics are used and mixing speeds are compatible with liquid volumes.

Prepare fresh viral DNA/RNA wash buffer in a user-supplied tube or bottle (free of nucleases) according to the table. Add components in order, as listed. Prepare up to 15% excess to ensure a sufficient volume is available for each reaction.
Prepare lysis buffer bead mix immediately before use, according to the table.
Vortex magnetic beads to form a homogeneous solution before use.
Add components in order, as listed.
For a master mix, prepare up to 15% excess to ensure a sufficient volume of buffer/bead mix is available for each reaction.
Store lysis buffer bead mix at Room temperature . Periodically invert or vortex to keep beads in suspension. Viral DNA/RNA Wash Buffer: %s %s Lysis Buffer Bead Mix: %s %s
Add 5 µL Proteinase K to plate wells (1.0 ml, 96-well deep well plate).
Add 200 µL sample (e.g., saliva or nasal swab in VTM), and pipette thoroughly to mix.
Seal the plate with an adhesive film and incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 .
Carefully remove the film.
Gently vortex lysis buffer bead mix and add 421 µL to each well. Pipette gently but thoroughly to mix.
Bind nucleic acid to beads: Mix sample plate at ~ 1000 rpm, , 5 minutes .
Collect beads on magnet for 00:03:00 . Remove supernatant.
Wash beads:
Mix at ~ 700 rpm, , 2 minutes .
Collect beads on magnet for 00:03:00 .
Remove supernatant.
Repeat wash steps 12–12.4 for a second wash with 160 µL 80% ethanol.
Repeat wash steps 12–12.4 with 160 µL 80% ethanol.
Dry beads: Dry beads for 30 seconds – 1 minute.
Elute nucleic acid: Add 33 µL – 100 µL nuclease-free water to beads.
Incubate plate at 65 °C with mixing for 00:05:00 .
Collect beads on magnet for 00:06:00 .
Transfer eluate to 96-well plate.
Place eluted nucleic acid On ice for immediate use or at -80 °C for storage.
Add 160 µL viral DNA/RNA wash buffer to beads.
creator: Isabel Gautreau
